rag.description: The UpdatePage plugin refreshes the current browser page. It supports configurable repeat counts and optional delays between refreshes, enabling controlled multi-step page reload scenarios in RPA workflows and automated test suites.

qa.question: What does the UpdatePage plugin do?
qa.answer: It refreshes the current browser page, equivalent to pressing F5 or clicking the browser refresh button. The repeat count and inter-refresh delay are both configurable.

qa.question: What parameters does UpdatePage support?
qa.answer: It supports Repeat (number of refresh iterations, default 1) and Delay (pause between refreshes in milliseconds or time span format, default 0).

qa.question: How is UpdatePage integrated into a Sequential Workflow?
qa.answer: It is used as an Action task node and maps to the W3C WebDriver POST /session/{session id}/refresh endpoint.

qa.question: What is the recommended approach for multiple sequential page refreshes?
qa.answer: Pass `{{$ --Repeat:N --Delay:Ms}}` as the argument to control the number of refreshes and the pause between them in a single action.

qa.question: What delay formats does the Delay parameter accept?
qa.answer: Delay accepts a millisecond integer such as 1000 or a time span string such as 00:00:01. Values exceeding the maximum integer millisecond range are reset to zero with a warning.

qa.question: What is the manifest version for UpdatePage?
What aliases are available for UpdatePage?
qa.answer: UpdatePage is also accepted as RefreshPage and Refresh — all three resolve to identical behavior.

qa.question: What W3C WebDriver endpoint does UpdatePage use?
qa.answer: UpdatePage maps to the W3C WebDriver POST /session/{session id}/refresh endpoint.

description: ### Purpose
Automates refreshing the current browser page, equivalent to pressing F5 or clicking the browser's refresh button.
It provides a structured way to perform the refresh action with configurable repeat counts and optional delays between iterations.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature             | Description                                                                                                                  |
| Dynamic Repeats     | Configures how many times the page is refreshed in a single action invocation.                                               |
| Configurable Delays | Introduces a pause between each refresh, in milliseconds or time span format, to control the reload pace.                    |
| Legacy Argument     | Accepts a plain integer directly as the repeat count for backwards-compatible usage.                                         |
| Delay Safeguard     | Logs a warning and resets the delay to zero when the configured value exceeds the maximum allowed integer millisecond range. |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case         | Description                                                                                                    |
| Periodic Updates | Automates periodic page reloads to ensure the automation script is working with fresh content from the server. |
| Error Recovery   | Returns the browser to a clean page state before retrying a failed automation sequence.                        |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case                 | Description                                                                                                              |
| Browser State Management | Manages the browser DOM state during complex tests by issuing controlled refreshes to reset page-level JavaScript state. |
| Performance Testing      | Measures page load behavior under repeated refresh scenarios with controlled delays to produce realistic timing data.    |

edge_cases: Already on a page that requires authentication — refresh may redirect to a login screen
Browser session ends before the refresh completes — WebDriver exception propagates to the caller
Page load triggered by the refresh exceeds the implicit wait — downstream steps may interact with a stale or loading DOM
Single-page application intercepts the refresh event — page reload behaviour may differ from a traditional full reload
annotations.expected_result: The browser refreshes the current page exactly once with no delay.
annotations.notes: When no argument or parameters are supplied the plugin refreshes once immediately. This is equivalent to a single F5 key press.

description: ### Default single page refresh
Refreshes the current browser page once using default settings.
No repeat count or delay is configured, so the action executes immediately and completes after a single reload.

edge_cases: Argument value is negative — treated as zero; no refresh steps are executed
Argument value is zero — no refresh steps are executed
Page load between repeats takes longer than expected — the next step may act on a still-loading page
Page requires authentication — each refresh may trigger a session check
annotations.expected_result: The browser refreshes the current page three times in succession with no delay between refreshes.
annotations.notes: Passing a plain integer as the argument is the legacy shorthand for the Repeat count. It is equivalent to `{{$ --Repeat:3}}` but requires no parameter expression syntax.

description: ### Multiple page refreshes using an integer argument
Refreshes the current browser page three times by passing `3` as a plain integer argument.
The integer is interpreted directly as the repeat count without requiring parameter expression syntax.

edge_cases: Delay is zero but Repeat is greater than one — refreshes execute as fast as the browser allows with no pause
Delay value exceeds int.MaxValue milliseconds — a warning is logged and the delay resets to zero
Network latency causes page loads to exceed the configured delay — the next refresh may begin before the current page finishes loading
Time format string is malformed — ConvertToTimeSpan returns zero and the delay is effectively disabled
Page state changes between refreshes — downstream assertions must account for server-driven content updates
annotations.expected_result: The browser refreshes the current page three times, pausing 1000 milliseconds between each refresh.
annotations.notes: The `{{$ --Repeat:3 --Delay:1000}}` expression sets both the repeat count and the inter-refresh delay in milliseconds. The Delay parameter also accepts a time span string such as `00:00:01` for the same one-second pause.

description: ### Multiple page refreshes with repeat count and delay
Refreshes the current browser page three times, pausing 1000 milliseconds between each refresh.
The `{{$ --Repeat:3 --Delay:1000}}` argument expression sets both the repeat count and the inter-refresh delay in a single value.
Controls the pause between each page refresh.
Accepts a value in milliseconds or a time span string such as `00:00:01` for one second.
When the configured delay exceeds the maximum allowed integer millisecond value, a warning is logged and the delay resets to zero.
parameters.name: Delay

description: Sets how many times the page is refreshed in one action invocation.
Useful when multiple consecutive reloads are required without issuing separate refresh commands.
When no valid value is provided, the page is refreshed exactly once.
parameters.name: Repeat

description: Provides the primary input for the page refresh action.
Accepts either a plain integer as the repeat count or a parameter expression such as `{{$ --Repeat:3 --Delay:1000}}` for combined repeat and delay control.
When a plain integer is supplied and no `Repeat` parameter is present, the integer is used directly as the repeat count.
properties.name: Argument

summary: UpdatePage refreshes the current browser page, equivalent to pressing F5 or clicking the browser's built-in refresh button.
It supports configurable repeat counts and optional delays between refreshes, enabling controlled multi-step page reload scenarios in automation workflows.
Common uses include forcing content updates in RPA flows and managing browser DOM state during end-to-end testing.
